title: Live Peer Development
expectation: Use a swarm worker checkout as the only mutable implementation surface for this process; do not perform code-changing work from the shared repository checkout. Follow the development-process blueprint through relevant blueprint updates, worker-backed feature-branch setup, persistent worker-terminal development, committed milestone updates, and live worker preview sharing with the user. The preview dashboard must run on a swarm worker EC2 instance, not on the manager instance, and should be exposed through a worker-specific tunnel URL that is distinct from the manager dashboard tunnel. Run the worker preview from the feature-branch checkout with the same dashboard setup shape as the manager, keep the work on the feature branch, expect continued user feedback while the preview is live, and stop before merge, release promotion, runtime deploy, and manager-host live validation unless the user explicitly switches to Full Development Process.
idlePrompt: If the current live peer development milestone is complete, reply only with 'done: live peer development complete'. If you are truly blocked on a user or environment dependency, reply only with 'blocked: live peer development blocked'. Otherwise resume the work immediately and perform the next required blueprint, implementation, verification, commit, preview, or validation step instead of giving only a status update.
stopConditions: blocked on user or environment dependency

steps.title: Set up the worker-backed feature-branch surface
Implement and refine the current milestone
Run and verify the worker preview
Share the preview and collect operator feedback
Commit the stable milestone on the feature branch
